## Shell/Bash-Specific Review Context

When reviewing shell scripts, pay particular attention to:

### Bash Validation Idioms (Do NOT Flag as Missing Validation)
- `${VAR:?message}` ‚Äî exits non-zero if VAR is unset or empty. This IS validation.
- `${VAR:-default}` ‚Äî provides a default. This IS null-safe handling.
- `set -euo pipefail` ‚Äî exits on error, unset vars, and pipe failures. This IS error handling.
- `trap 'cleanup' EXIT` ‚Äî guaranteed cleanup on all exit paths. This IS resource management.
- `local` variables ‚Äî scoped to functions, not global namespace pollution.

### Common Shell Bugs
- Unquoted variable expansions ‚Äî `$VAR` vs `"$VAR"` in word-splitting contexts
- Missing `|| true` on commands that may fail inside `set -e` when failure is expected
- `echo` vs `printf` ‚Äî `echo` interprets escape sequences inconsistently across platforms
- Array expansion without quotes ‚Äî `${arr[@]}` vs `"${arr[@]}"`
- `[[ ]]` vs `[ ]` ‚Äî prefer `[[ ]]` for bash; `[ ]` has word splitting issues
- TOCTOU race conditions on temp files ‚Äî always use `mktemp`
- Relying on `$?` after a command inside `set -e` ‚Äî the script may have already exited

### Security (Shell-specific)
- Command injection via unvalidated variables in `eval`, backticks, or `$()` with user input
- Unquoted variables in `rm`, `mv`, `cp` ‚Äî glob expansion risk
- `curl | bash` patterns ‚Äî never pipe untrusted URLs to shell
- Temp files in predictable locations without `mktemp` ‚Äî symlink attacks
- Secrets in command-line arguments ‚Äî visible in `/proc/*/cmdline` and `ps`
- Heredocs and variable expansion ‚Äî use `<<'EOF'` (quoted) to prevent expansion of secrets

### Data Flow Trust Boundaries
- `git diff` file paths ‚Äî **untrusted**; PR authors control filenames. Safe to display, unsafe to pass to `eval`, `source`, or unquoted shell expansions.
- `git diff` line numbers ‚Äî trusted; generated by git internals, not user-controlled.
- `jq` extracted fields from LLM/API responses ‚Äî untrusted; validate types and ranges before arithmetic or command use.
- GitHub Actions env vars set by the runner from repo metadata (`GITHUB_REPOSITORY`, `GITHUB_SHA`, `GITHUB_RUN_ID`) ‚Äî trusted.
- GitHub Actions env vars derived from PR content (`PR_TITLE`, `PR_BODY`, `GITHUB_HEAD_REF` on forks) ‚Äî **untrusted**; contain PR-author free text.
- User-authored PR content (titles, descriptions, comments) ‚Äî untrusted free text.

### Portability
- `grep -P` (PCRE) is not available on all platforms ‚Äî prefer `grep -E` (ERE)
- `sed -i` differs between GNU and BSD ‚Äî use temp file + mv for portability
- `readarray`/`mapfile` require bash 4+ ‚Äî not available on macOS default bash 3.2
- Process substitution `<(cmd)` requires bash, not POSIX sh
